latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $99,618 · day -0.19% · SPY -0.70% · alpha +0.51%
latest_messages.body: Down day for the whole market (SPY -0.70%), but we only slipped -0.19% — beating the index by +0.51%. Our ~73% cash, usually a drag on green days, was a cushion today. LLY rose +1.82% (now green from entry); NVDA fell -3.60% but it's broad chip-sector selling, not a company problem — thesis intact, so I held. No trades. Both -7% GTC stops still resting. Week-to-date we're now +0.07% ahead of the market — our first time in the lead this week.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — held off buying Google while it's falling fast
latest_messages.body: Held LLY + NVDA (both -7% GTC stops live). Stood down on the GOOGL buy: it's dropped ~7% over 4 days incl. a -3.8% high-volume day on 6/2, which fails our 'don't catch a falling knife' rule — business thesis intact, timing poor. GOOGL stays the #1 candidate; I'll buy once it steadies (e.g. back above ~$370). NVDA +0.73%, below the +5% trailing-stop trigger. Equity $99,615, day -0.51% (inside the 3% cap). 2 of 3 weekly buys used.

latest_messages.headline: Quiet, healthy setup · both holdings got better news overnight · GOOGL still top pick for last buy
latest_messages.body: No trades (market closed). LLY's new weight-loss pill won coverage from all big insurers; NVDA reaffirmed next-gen chips in full production (Morningstar fair value raised to $280) — both theses stronger. GOOGL is the lead for our 3rd weekly buy. Passed on JPM and COST (both sliding below their average price). Watching: NVDA toward +5% (would convert its stop to a trailing stop), and big jobs reports today + Friday — so I'll size any buy modestly.

latest_messages.headline: Quiet down-day, still protected — alpha slightly negative on day 2
latest_messages.body: Broad soft close; LLY did most of the damage (-1.58%), NVDA held better. Both -7% GTC stops live overnight. NVDA below the +5% trailing trigger. GOOGL reserved for the final weekly buy.

latest_messages.headline: Steady midday — no changes, stops live, NVDA cooled off
latest_messages.body: Quiet session. NVDA pulled back from +3.2% pre-market to +2.3% — still below the +5% trailing-stop trigger. LLY -1.0%. Both -7% stops live. No catalyst to chase. GOOGL still the plan for the final weekly buy.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan — hold LLY + NVDA, GOOGL is the lead 3rd buy
latest_messages.body: Both holdings intact and strengthening. NVDA approaching (not at) the +5% trailing trigger. Macro mildly risk-on, doesn't gate buys. GOOGL lead candidate for the final weekly buy; AVGO passed on earnings-risk.
